tables.title: Types of Pediatric Shock
rows: Loss of circulating volume (hemorrhage or dehydration)
Trauma with blood loss, surgical hemorrhage, severe dehydration (vomiting/diarrhea), burns
Tachycardia, weak pulses, flat neck veins, cool/mottled skin, prolonged CRT, decreased UOP
Isotonic crystalloid bolus 10–20 mL/kg; blood products if hemorrhagic; identify and stop source of loss
Bacterial sepsis (meningococcus, E. coli, GAS), viral sepsis, fungal infections
Early: warm/flushed skin, brisk CRT, tachycardia, fever; Late: cool/mottled, prolonged CRT, hypotension
Fluid resuscitation + broad-spectrum antibiotics within 60 min + vasoactive support if fluid-refractory
IgE-mediated mast cell degranulation → vasodilation + bronchospasm
Food allergies (nuts, milk), insect stings, medications, latex
Urticaria, angioedema, wheezing, tachycardia, hypotension, stridor, abdominal pain
Epinephrine 0.01 mg/kg IM (max 0.3 mg adult) + isotonic fluids + antihistamines + corticosteroids
Loss of sympathetic tone from spinal cord injury
Cervical/thoracic spinal cord injury (typically above T6)
Hypotension with BRADYCARDIA, warm dry skin (poorly responsive to fluids)
IV fluids, atropine for bradycardia, vasopressors, spinal immobilization
Pump failure — the heart cannot generate adequate output
Myocarditis, congenital heart disease, cardiac toxins, post-cardiac arrest, arrhythmias
Gallop rhythm, hepatomegaly, pulmonary edema (crackles), tachycardia, weak pulses, poor perfusion
Cautious fluid boluses (may worsen pulmonary edema), vasoactive support (epinephrine, milrinone), inotropes
Physical obstruction to cardiac filling or output
Tension pneumothorax, cardiac tamponade, massive pulmonary embolism
Hypotension, JVD (or flat veins if hypovolemic component), muffled heart sounds (tamponade), tracheal deviation (tension pneumo)
Relieve the obstruction: needle decompression, pericardiocentesis, or embolectomy + fluids

tables.title: Stages of Pediatric Shock
rows: Prolonged CRT (>2 sec), weak pulses, cool/mottled skin
Poor CRT (>4 sec), thready pulses, cyanosis, marked mottling
No palpable pulses, cold extremities, ashen/gray

tables.title: Hypotension Thresholds by Age
rows: SBP < 90 mmHg (or adult formula if applicable)
